name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
tagline: A climate-controlled cathedral with a camera-shutter roof, hosting eight matches that climax in a July 15 World Cup semifinal.

why.title: The Pinwheel Roof
why.text: Eight translucent triangular panels slide open like a camera aperture in about eight minutes. Nothing else in sports looks like it.

why.title: Halo Board
why.text: A 360° video ring wraps the entire roof rim, the size of three NFL jumbotrons. Every seat sees everything.

why.title: Semifinal Stage
why.text: One of only two semifinals worldwide, feeding the final at MetLife. Atlanta is the last stop before the throne.

why.title: Soccer City
why.text: Atlanta United routinely packs 70,000-plus and shatters MLS attendance records. This town shows up loud.

gettingThere: The stadium sits right in downtown Atlanta, a mile from Centennial Olympic Park. Skip the car — MARTA rail drops you at the doorstep.
transit: MARTA rail is the move: GWCC/CNN Center station is essentially at the gates, Vine City is a 10-min walk and dodges traffic
From Hartsfield-Jackson airport (ATL), take the Red/Gold line to Five Points and transfer to Blue/Green — about $2.50 each way
Official GWCC parking decks and lots exist but expect surge pricing and gridlock on match days; reserve ahead if you must drive
Rideshare zones are on Northside Dr and Broad St, but post-match surge waits are brutal — MARTA clears out faster
matchday: The official FIFA Fan Festival takes over Centennial Olympic Park downtown — free entry, a 40-ft screen, concerts, and food vendors across 18 days from June 11. The Home Depot Backyard, an 11-acre green west of the stadium, is the tailgate hub.
food: Fan First pricing is the real headline: $2 refillable sodas, $5 beers, all whole-dollar with tax baked in — cheapest in U.S. sports
Best of Atlanta stands bring Chick-fil-A and The Varsity inside at street prices
670+ points of sale, fully cashless, so lines move fast
Outside the gates, hit West Midtown and the BeltLine's Ponce City Market for breweries and nightlife
tips: Take MARTA, not a car — Vine City or GWCC stations beat parking and rideshare surge every time
Eat and drink inside; the whole-dollar Fan First menu is unbeatable, no need to overspend outside
Arrive early for World Cup security and bring a clear bag — the bag policy is strict, or go bag-free
Aim for the lower-bowl supporters' ends behind the goals for the loudest atmosphere
The roof stays closed and AC is on, so dress for indoor comfort — but pack a poncho for the muggy walk to transit
Hit the free Fan Festival at Centennial Park even without a ticket; pre-register and walk over from downtown
weather: June-July Atlanta is hot and humid with highs near 88°F and pop-up afternoon storms, but the roof stays closed and the stadium is air-conditioned — pack a packable poncho for the walk and dress light.
lore: FIFA strips the Mercedes-Benz name during the tournament, branding it 'Atlanta Stadium' — but the iconic roof star stays put
Built for ~$1.6 billion and opened in 2017, it was the first LEED Platinum pro sports stadium in the U.S.
Hosted Super Bowl LIII, two College Football Playoff title games, and the 2024 Copa América opener
Atlanta United won the 2018 MLS Cup here in front of 73,019 — a championship roar this city won't forget

food.name: Fox Bros. Bar-B-Q
food.note: Big Tex brisket sandwich and loaded Totchos

food.name: Sublime Doughnuts
food.note: fan-favorite handcrafted doughnuts for the sweet tooth
why: Handcrafted sweet to fuel the match

pre.name: FIFA Fan Festival
pre.where: Centennial Olympic Park, ~10-min walk or one MARTA stop from the stadium

pre.name: No Mas! Cantina
pre.note: Mexican fare, 50-ft travertine bar, big patio
why: Walkable Mexican feast near the gates

pre.name: Atlantucky Brewing
pre.note: Nappy Roots' Black-owned brewery, art gallery, patio

pre.name: Der Biergarten
pre.note: authentic German beer hall, steins and brats
why: Steins and brats for soccer fans

pre.name: Hudson Grille
pre.where: 120 Marietta St NW, downtown near Centennial Park
pre.note: huge draft list, every game on screens

post.name: Wild Leap
post.where: 125 Ted Turner Dr SW, downtown two-level taproom
post.note: slushie flights, chill multi-level patio post-game

post.name: Park Bar
post.where: 150 Walton St NW, across from Centennial Park
post.note: dim tavern, beer, burgers, Bruce Hornsby

post.name: Nest on Four
post.where: 159 Northside Dr NW, Signia by Hilton, level 4

post.name: STATS Brewpub
post.where: 300 Marietta St NW, downtown Luckie-Marietta District
post.note: 70+ HD screens, house beer poured from the tables
why: Premier downtown sports bar near the gates

merch.name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Official Team Store
merch.where: behind Gate 1, section 102; 8,000 sq ft two-level flagship (event days only, ticket required)

merch.name: In-stadium retail kiosks (Fanatics-run)
merch.where: 15 retail points throughout the concourses, incl. stands near Gates 2-3

merch.name: Official Team Store at Atlantic Station (Falcons/United, Fanatics-run)
merch.where: 260 18th St NW, Suite 10100, Atlantic Station; relocated flagship store (also carries WC26-related gear)

transport.summary: The stadium sits in downtown Atlanta with two MARTA rail stations at its doorstep, and the venue heavily pushes MARTA over driving. On match days MARTA boosts trains to five-minute headways and rideshare drop-offs are pushed to two designated zones away from the gates.

rail.name: MARTA Blue/Green Line
rail.detail: One stop west of SEC District; a covered pedestrian bridge leads straight to the stadium gates (~10-min walk), and it dodges the heaviest crowds. Widely cited as the best traffic-avoidance choice.
rail.deal: $2.50 each way; tap-to-pay with credit/debit or mobile wallet at the gates

rail.name: MARTA Blue/Green Line
rail.detail: Renamed the Sports, Entertainment and Convention (SEC) District Station; essentially at the stadium doorstep and the venue's officially preferred stop.
rail.deal: $2.50 each way; reusable Breeze card is $2 plus fare; limited World Cup Breeze cards issued

rail.name: MARTA Red/Gold Line (airport link)
rail.detail: From Hartsfield-Jackson (ATL), board Red or Gold northbound (~15 min to Five Points), then transfer to a westbound Blue/Green train ~2 stops to Vine City (~5 min).
rail.deal: $2.50 flat fare covers the whole airport-to-stadium trip incl. transfer

shuttle.name: Fetii Gameday Shuttle
shuttle.detail: Park at Atlantic Station and ride a ~15-20 min shuttle from 2 hours pre-game to 90 min post-game. Note: this runs for Falcons games — verify availability for World Cup matches; not wheelchair accessible.

transport.fromAirport: Hartsfield-Jackson (ATL) is ~10 miles south and connects directly by MARTA rail: take the Red or Gold line from inside the terminal to Five Points (~15 min), transfer to a westbound Blue/Green train to Vine City or SEC District (~5 min). Total fare is a flat $2.50. Late-night riders should confirm the train sign reads 'Airport.'
transport.rideshare: Direct drop-offs at the stadium, GWCC, and Centennial Olympic Park are prohibited on match days. Use Zone 1 (Northside Dr at GWCC Bus Lane C, best for Gate 1, ~10-min walk) or Zone 2 (Broad St between Trinity & Mitchell, best for Gates 2-4 with quicker airport/I-20/I-75-85 access). Expect heavy post-match surge waits — MARTA usually clears out faster.
transport.bike: Free bike valet for stadium events, located between Gates 3 and 4. Verify operation for World Cup matchdays.
tips: MARTA is the move and is voted #1 by fans — five-minute train headways run until 10:30 p.m. on match days
Tap-and-pay at the fare gates with any credit/debit card or mobile wallet to skip Breeze kiosk lines
Expect rolling downtown road closures and VIP motorcade stoppages — build in extra time and avoid driving through the core
Vine City's covered pedestrian bridge keeps you out of the sun/rain on the walk to the gates

parking.summary: Parking is fully cashless and must be reserved and paid in advance — there is no drive-up or cash parking on match days, and cars without a prepaid digital pass are turned away from stadium-adjacent lots. The venue strongly steers fans to MARTA instead.

lots.name: GWCC Red Deck
lots.price: ~$50+ on high-demand days; expect $50-$100+ for World Cup
lots.detail: Covered Georgia World Congress Center deck close to the stadium; reserve via the official portal.

lots.name: GWCC Silver Deck
lots.price: ~$50+ on high-demand days; World Cup pricing higher
lots.detail: Covered GWCC deck near the stadium; subject to availability around concurrent GWCC events.

lots.name: Surface lots (e.g. Yellow/Orange)
lots.detail: Surface and overflow lots around the GWCCA campus; closest/premium spots command the top prices on big match days.

parking.prepaid: All parking is pre-purchase only through the official ParkHub-powered system at parking.mercedesbenzstadium.com (also gwccparking.com). Buy early — World Cup demand pushes official lots to $50-$100+. Use caution with secondary/resale parking passes.
parking.accessible: ADA parking is first-come within the decks and lots (no separate reservation tier widely advertised). An ADA shuttle drops near Vine City MARTA by Gate 1, and Mobility Ambassadors provide entry-to-seat escort. Note the pedestrian bridge has drawn criticism for steep ramps, so MARTA plus a Mobility Ambassador escort may be easier for wheelchair users.
parking.tailgating: The Home Depot Backyard, an 11-acre green space on the former Georgia Dome site just west of the stadium, is the tailgate hub. Confirm World Cup-specific access and any lot/closure changes closer to matches.
tips: No walk-up or cash parking on match days — buy a digital pass in advance or you'll be turned away
Official lots will run $50-$100+ for World Cup matches, so MARTA at $2.50 is far cheaper and faster out
GWCC deck availability can be squeezed by other Georgia World Congress Center events — reserve as early as possible
Avoid secondary-market parking passes; buy only through the official portal
